title: VCreateCalibration Statement
description: VCreateCalibration creates a vision calibration at runtime.
notes:
Here are the basic steps to create a runtime calibration:

	Execute VCreateCalibration.
	Use VSet to set CameraOrientation and TargetSequence
	 properties.  Set other properties as necessary.
	If the camera is not Standalone, you must
	 create a point file for the calibration using the same name, or call
	 VCalPoints to teach calibration points.
	Execute VCal to run the calibration.
	Set the Calibration property for one or more
	 sequences that will use the new calibration.
	Execute VSave to save the changes.

To make changes to the vision configuration permanent, you must call VSave.
examples:
Function CreateCal String cal$ cal$ = "mycal" VCreateCalibration cal$ VSet cal$.CameraOrientation, VISION_CAMORIENT_MOBILEJ2 VSet cal$.TargetSequence, "calSeq" VCalPoints cal$ VCal cal$ VSave
Fend
